column17: Chart Designer: Dr. Thomas R. Nicely

column2: The Cincinnati Bengals got off to a horrible start by losing 6 of their first 7 games, as Cincinnati Bengals Quarterback Ken Anderson struggled with too many interceptions due to a young offensive line struggling to protect him.  The Cincinnati Bengals would finish the season strong wining 6 of their final 9, but with a disappointing 7-9 record, Bengals Coach Forrest Gregg resigns after the season and former Cincinnati Bengals Quarterback Sam Wyche is hired as Head Coach of the Cincinnati Bengals.  Despite the losing record, the Bengals claimed the top overall defense in the NFL. Cris Collinsworth was one of the few bright spots of the season gaining 1130 in receiving yards.
